description: Multi-surface Phase 6 on a desktop app leg: ONE parallel startSurface array opens a native Windows app (Character Map, the app-surfaces-a1 target) and a background process concurrently — the app lane and the process lane overlap in one step. Both surfaces are then driven by name (click/type on the app; type on the REPL), and one array closeSurface tears both down. Gated to Windows hosts with charmap.exe present; elsewhere the app preflight lands the context as SKIPPED.

tests.description: A parallel array opens charmap (app lane) and node -i (process lane) in one step; each is addressable by its descriptor name afterwards, and the array closeSurface ends both.

steps.description: The app surface from the array is fully drivable
The process surface from the same array is a real, typable process
One array close tears down both kinds
